features.summary: Apple reported record Q1 FY2022 results with revenue of $123.9 billion, up 11% year-over-year, driven by strong performance across most product categories. iPhone set an all-time revenue record at $71.6 billion despite supply constraints, while Mac achieved $10.9 billion in revenue (+25% YoY) powered by M1 chip adoption. Services reached a record $19.5 billion (+24% YoY) with 785 million paid subscriptions, up 165 million from the prior year. The company's active installed base grew to 1.8 billion devices, setting records across all major product categories and geographic segments.
features.management_outlook: Management expects solid year-over-year revenue growth and a March quarter record despite continued supply constraints, which should be less severe than December quarter levels. Revenue growth is expected to decelerate due to a challenging 54% growth comparison from the prior year March quarter and a 3-point foreign exchange headwind. Services growth is expected to remain strong double-digit but decelerate from December performance due to tougher comparisons from pandemic-driven usage increases last year. The company maintains optimism about long-term iPhone growth potential, citing early stages of 5G adoption and continued installed base expansion.
features.risks: Supply chain constraints remain significant, particularly affecting legacy chip nodes, though expected to improve modestly in the March quarter. Foreign exchange headwinds are anticipated to create a 2-point negative impact on growth. COVID-19 continues to create uncertainty with potential business impacts if conditions worsen beyond current projections. Inflationary pressures are affecting costs across logistics and other operational areas, with unclear duration of these elevated expense levels.
features.key_drivers: Strong iPhone 13 family demand drove record iPhone revenue despite constraints, with high customer satisfaction (98%) and market share gains across multiple regions. M1 chip transition success powered Mac growth with record upgrader activity and strong enterprise adoption. Services growth was broad-based across all categories including cloud, music, video, advertising, and payments, supported by expanding paid subscriptions and increasing customer engagement. The growing installed base of 1.8 billion active devices provides a strong foundation for continued services monetization and ecosystem expansion.
